title: Supporting refugees and asylum seekers :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Supporting refugees and asylum seekers
Each year, people arrive in New Zealand as refugees through the Refugee Quota Programme, the Refugee Family Support Category, and other programmes or humanitarian interventions.
- Refugee and protection unit The Refugee and Protection Unit promotes the successful resettlement of refugees in New Zealand. We work with government and international agencies to help refugees who arrive here under our quota. This is part of New Zealand’s Refugee Resettlement Strategy.

## Refugee and protection unit
The Refugee and Protection Unit promotes the successful resettlement of refugees in New Zealand. We work with government and international agencies to help refugees who arrive here under our quota. This is part of New Zealand’s Refugee Resettlement Strategy.
- Refugee resettlement strategy Immigration New Zealand works with others to deliver improved settlement outcomes for former refugees.

## Refugee resettlement strategy
Immigration New Zealand works with others to deliver improved settlement outcomes for former refugees.
- National Refugee Resettlement Forum Each year, the National Refugee Resettlement Forum provides refugee resettlement stakeholders with the opportunity to actively engage on strategic refugee resettlement issues.

## National Refugee Resettlement Forum
Each year, the National Refugee Resettlement Forum provides refugee resettlement stakeholders with the opportunity to actively engage on strategic refugee resettlement issues.
- The National Leadership Coordination Service Programme The Refugee Health National Leadership Coordination Service (NLCS) was commissioned by Health New Zealand (Te Whatu Ora) in 2023 and is delivered by the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE).

## The National Leadership Coordination Service Programme
The Refugee Health National Leadership Coordination Service (NLCS) was commissioned by Health New Zealand (Te Whatu Ora) in 2023 and is delivered by the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE).
- Refugee Advisory Panel The Refugee Advisory Panel works to support government decision-makers and policy developers on matters impacting refugees in New Zealand.

## Refugee Advisory Panel
The Refugee Advisory Panel works to support government decision-makers and policy developers on matters impacting refugees in New Zealand.
- Improving our processes for asylum seekers In 2021 an independent review was commissioned about the detention of asylum claimants. The review made 11 recommendations and Immigration New Zealand accepted all of these.

## Improving our processes for asylum seekers
In 2021 an independent review was commissioned about the detention of asylum claimants. The review made 11 recommendations and Immigration New Zealand accepted all of these.
- Welcoming Communities Welcoming Communities is a programme led by Immigration New Zealand (INZ) in partnership with the Ministry of Ethnic Communities and the Human Rights Commission. Welcoming Communities works towards healthier, happier and more productive communities by welcoming newcomers into the local community.

## Welcoming Communities
Welcoming Communities is a programme led by Immigration New Zealand (INZ) in partnership with the Ministry of Ethnic Communities and the Human Rights Commission. Welcoming Communities works towards healthier, happier and more productive communities by welcoming newcomers into the local community.
- New Zealand Refugee Health Scholarship Programme The New Zealand Refugee Health Scholarship supports former refugees and their children to study health-related qualifications in New Zealand. It aims to increase representation of refugee-background communities in the health workforce and improve health outcomes.

## New Zealand Refugee Health Scholarship Programme
The New Zealand Refugee Health Scholarship supports former refugees and their children to study health-related qualifications in New Zealand. It aims to increase representation of refugee-background communities in the health workforce and improve health outcomes.
